chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: 2 or more (at least one must be delusions, hallucinations, or disorganized speech)
duration_rule: 6 months continuous evidence (1 month active-phase symptoms)

symptoms.description: The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient exhibits speech that is noticeably incoherent, disorganized, or frequently derails from the topic.
The patient exhibits severely abnormal psychomotor behavior, ranging from unresponsiveness to agitated, purposeless movement.
The patient displays negative symptoms, such as diminished emotional expression, restricted affect, or a lack of volition.
The patient experiences clinically significant impairment in occupational, academic, or social functioning due to their symptoms.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: At least 2 (at least one must be delusions, hallucinations, or disorganized speech)

symptoms.description: The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient exhibits speech that is noticeably incoherent, disorganized, or frequently derails from the topic.
The patient exhibits severely abnormal psychomotor behavior, ranging from unresponsiveness to agitated, purposeless movement.
The patient displays negative symptoms, such as diminished emotional expression, restricted affect, or a lack of volition.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: Principal symptoms of schizophrenia coexist with a major mood episode; delusions/hallucinations for 2 weeks in absence of mood episode

symptoms.description: The patient has experienced delusions or hallucinations for two or more weeks in the absence of a major mood episode during the lifetime duration of the illness.
The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: At least 1 (delusions, hallucinations, disorganized speech, or behavior)
duration_rule: 1 day to 1 month (full return to baseline)

symptoms.description: The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient exhibits speech that is noticeably incoherent, disorganized, or frequently derails from the topic.
The patient exhibits severely abnormal psychomotor behavior, ranging from unresponsiveness to agitated, purposeless movement.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: 1 or more delusions; no other symptoms of schizophrenia

symptoms.description: The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

diagnosis_name: Psychotic Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
duration_rule: Not specified (physiological consequence of medical condition)

symptoms.description: The patient has a disturbance that is shown by history, physical examination, or laboratory findings to be the direct pathophysiological consequence of another medical condition.
The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: Hallucinations or delusions predominant in clinical picture
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient has symptoms that developed during or soon after substance intoxication, withdrawal, or medication exposure, with evidence that the substance is capable of producing the observed hallucinations or delusions.
The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Catatonia Associated with Another Mental Disorder (Specifier)
chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits stupor, characterized by a lack of psychomotor activity and unresponsiveness to the environment.
The patient maintains a rigid, uncomfortable posture against gravity even when told it is not necessary to do so.
The patient exhibits waxy flexibility, actively resisting attempts to change their position before yielding to pressure.
The patient exhibits an absence of, or very little, verbal response despite the evident physical ability to speak.
The patient exhibits motiveless resistance to instructions or attempts to be moved.
The patient voluntarily assumes and maintains an unnatural or uncomfortable pose.
The patient performs repeated movements that seem to have a goal but are excessive for the purpose.
The patient engages in repetitive, abnormally frequent, non-goal-directed movements.
The patient exhibits excessive motor activity that appears to have neither a purpose nor an external cause.
The patient exhibits facial contortions or grimacing not made in response to a noxious stimulus.
The patient exhibits verbatim, automatic repetition of someone else's words.
The patient automatically mimics or imitates another person's physical behavior.

diagnosis_name: Catatonic Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: At least 3 prominent symptoms of catatonia
duration_rule: Not specified (physiological consequence of medical condition)

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits stupor, characterized by a lack of psychomotor activity and unresponsiveness to the environment.
The patient maintains a rigid, uncomfortable posture against gravity even when told it is not necessary to do so.
The patient exhibits waxy flexibility, actively resisting attempts to change their position before yielding to pressure.
The patient exhibits an absence of, or very little, verbal response despite the evident physical ability to speak.
The patient exhibits motiveless resistance to instructions or attempts to be moved.
The patient voluntarily assumes and maintains an unnatural or uncomfortable pose.
The patient performs repeated movements that seem to have a goal but are excessive for the purpose.
The patient engages in repetitive, abnormally frequent, non-goal-directed movements.
The patient exhibits excessive motor activity that appears to have neither a purpose nor an external cause.
The patient exhibits facial contortions or grimacing not made in response to a noxious stimulus.
The patient exhibits verbatim, automatic repetition of someone else's words.
The patient automatically mimics or imitates another person's physical behavior.
